---
title: "Local Integration Testing"
subtitle: "R unit tests, pytest, fixtures, and integration test collection"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

The test suite is organized around **integration tests as the primary evaluation
metric**. Integration tests run against real pipeline output and catch bugs that
static fixtures cannot — GCTA marker exclusions, Hive partition path construction,
DuckDB view registration, Nextflow channel wiring, and schema drift between what
R writes and what downstream queries read. Unit tests support them by pinning
pure-computation correctness (hash determinism, ANOVA golden values, threshold
math) where no real pipeline output is needed or helpful.

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'themeVariables': {'fontSize': '16px'}}}%%
flowchart TD
    classDef primary fill:#48b96e,stroke:#2d8150,color:#fff
    classDef supporting fill:#7ab0d4,stroke:#4a80a4,color:#fff
    classDef gate fill:#e07b3a,stroke:#a3572a,color:#fff
    classDef data fill:#f5f5f5,stroke:#999,color:#333

    A([Rscript tests/run_tests.R]):::data
    B{TEST_DB_DIR set?}:::gate
    C[Unit tests — spec and regression guards]:::supporting
    D[Integration tests — primary evaluation]:::primary
    E[pytest tests/pytest/]:::supporting
    F([Results reported]):::data

    A --> B
    B -- No --> C --> F
    B -- Yes --> C --> F
    B -- Yes --> D --> F
    A --> E --> F
```


## Test Tiers

| Tier | Files | Role | Requirements | Skips when |
|------|-------|------|-------------|------------|
| **Integration** | `test-db_structure.R`, `test-cross_validation.R`, `test-assessment_cross_validation.R` | **Primary** — evaluate pipeline correctness with real output | `TEST_DB_DIR`, `TEST_WORK_DIR`, `TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT`, `TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT` | Env vars unset |
| **CV Pool** | `test-cv_pool.R` | **Scenario-specific** — validate non-marker causal variant handling | `TEST_DB_DIR`, `TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT`, `TEST_CV_POOL=true` | `TEST_CV_POOL` not set |
| Unit | all other `test-*.R` | Supporting — pin pure-computation correctness | Static fixtures only; no pipeline run needed | — |
| Python unit | `tests/pytest/test_create_causal_vars.py` | Supporting — validate Python scripts | `pytest`, `numpy`, `pandas` in Python env | `pytest` not installed |

Run all tiers with a single command; integration tests skip automatically when
their environment variables are absent:

```bash
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```


## Integration Tests

Integration tests are the primary evaluation metric for pipeline correctness.
They run against real pipeline output produced by `tests/collect_test_data.sh`
and are the authoritative check that the pipeline is working correctly end-to-end.
Unit tests check isolated R functions with static fixtures; integration tests catch
the broader class of bugs that only appear with real GCTA output — edge cases in
file format parsing, Hive partition path construction, DuckDB view registration,
Nextflow channel wiring, and schema drift between what R writes and what queries read.

### `test-db_structure.R` (27 tests)

Verifies the Parquet database written by `DB_MIGRATION_*` modules. Requires `TEST_DB_DIR`.

| Test group | What it checks |
|-----------|---------------|
| Directory structure | `markers/`, `mappings/`, `traits/`, `traits/causal_variants/`, `traits/phenotypes/` all exist |
| Marker set files | Parquet files exist; filenames derived from metadata hash lookup |
| Hive partitions | `population=` / `mapping_id=` directories exist with `data.parquet` files |
| Metadata files | `mappings_metadata.parquet` and `marker_set_metadata.parquet` present |
| Marker set metadata | Required columns; `strainfile_hash` is 64-char SHA-256; `strain_list` non-empty |
| Marker set schema | Correct 6-column schema; FK columns absent from data files |
| Mappings metadata | Core columns present; algorithm values `"inbred"`/`"loco"`; both PCA flags present; mapping IDs are 20-char hex |
| Genotype matrix schema | Long-format schema: `marker_set_id utf8`, `CHROM utf8`, `POS int32`, `strain utf8`, `allele float64` |
| Trait metadata schema | 13 columns including `nqtl int32`, `sim_seed int32`, `h2 float64`; `trait_id utf8` |
| Phenotype schema | `strain`/`phenotype` columns; no NA phenotype values |
| Causal variants schema | 6 columns; `QTL` values in `CHROM:POS` format |
| **Inbred marker invariant** | `meta$n_markers == marker_set_metadata$n_markers` — GCTA processes all markers for inbred |
| **Loco marker invariant** | `meta$n_markers < marker_set_metadata$n_markers` — GCTA silently excludes near-singular markers from loco output (issue111 regression guard) |
| Database queryability | `open_mapping_db()`, DuckDB table listing, `COUNT(*)` query, `db_stats()` |
| Mapping data integrity | All partitions non-empty; P ∈ (0, 1]; no NULL P; `var.exp` absent or all-NA |
| Query functions | `query_mapping_data()` returns joined data with CHROM/POS; `get_threshold_params()` returns valid BF and EIGEN thresholds |

### `test-cross_validation.R` (8 tests)

Compares DB Parquet contents against raw GWA files in the Nextflow work directory.
Requires `TEST_DB_DIR` and `TEST_WORK_DIR`.

| Test | What it checks |
|------|---------------|
| P and BETA round-trip | DB values match raw `.fastGWA`/`.mlma` files to 1e-12 tolerance |
| SE round-trip | DB SE values match raw source |
| AF1 round-trip | DB AF1 values match raw source |
| EIGEN threshold | DB `n_independent_tests` matches the pipeline EIGEN file |
| GWA column normalization | `read_raw_gwa_file()` returns 9 normalized columns (`marker`, `CHROM`, `POS`, `A1`, `A2`, `AF1`, `BETA`, `SE`, `P`) for both `.fastGWA` and `.mlma` formats |
| Full coverage | `query_by_mapping_id()` returns a non-empty result for every `mapping_id` in metadata |
| Marker count consistency | `meta$n_markers` matches actual partition row counts |
| **Loco/inbred invariant** | `loco$n_markers < inbred$n_markers` for the same marker set — regression guard documenting the GCTA exclusion behavior (issue111) |

### `test-assessment_cross_validation.R` (12 tests)

Compares `db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv` (DB path) against
`simulation_assessment_results.tsv` (legacy path). Requires `TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT`
and `TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT`.

| Test | What it checks |
|------|---------------|
| File existence | Both assessment TSVs present and non-empty |
| Hash determinism | `generate_mapping_id()` produces identical 20-char hash across two independent calls |
| **Column completeness** | DB assessment has exactly 35 columns matching the `format_assessment_tsv()` schema |
| **`calculate_simrep_performance()`** | Runs without error on real data; Power and FDR are in [0, 1] |
| **`designate_qtl()` categories** | Only the 4 valid designations (`Detected.CV`, `Missed.CV`, `CV.Not.Significant.In.Interval`, `False.Discovery`) appear in real data |
| Detection concordance | Detected flag agreement between DB and legacy paths is 1.0 for all mappings |
| Designation concordance | `designate_qtl()` category agreement is 1.0 for all mappings |
| Interval concordance | Jaccard overlap of `[startPOS, endPOS]` intervals is 1.0 for all detected QTLs |
| VarExp populated | DB assessment has at least some non-NA `Simulated.QTL.VarExp` values |
| VarExp concordance | `Simulated.QTL.VarExp` matches between DB and legacy paths within 1e-6 |
| **Per-population concordance** | Detection concordance holds independently within each `strain_set_id` (multi-population guard) |
| **Chromosome name consistency** | `CHROM` portion of `QTL` column matches between DB and legacy paths (multi-species guard) |

### `test-cv_pool.R` (7 tests)

Validates the `test_cv_pool` scenario where `cv_maf < ms_maf`, producing causal
variants absent from GWA output. Requires `TEST_DB_DIR`, `TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT`, and
`TEST_CV_POOL=true`. **Do not set** `TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT` for this scenario —
the legacy path is incompatible (non-marker causal variants are invisible to it).

| Test | What it checks |
|------|---------------|
| Causal genotypes directory | `traits/causal_genotypes/` exists with non-empty parquet files |
| Causal genotype schema | `(trait_id, QTL, CHROM, POS, strain, allele)` columns present |
| Non-marker FN rows | Assessment output contains rows with `Simulated=TRUE, log10p=NA` |
| `significant=NA` pattern | Non-marker FN rows have `significant=NA` (not `FALSE`) — distinguishes "absent from GWA" |
| VarExp for non-marker rows | `Simulated.QTL.VarExp` is populated for non-marker FN rows (per-trait causal genotype path) |
| Non-marker in causal but not markers | Causal QTLs absent from DuckDB markers view — confirms superset pool was exercised |
| `designate_qtl()` classification | Non-marker FN rows (`significant=NA`) classified as `Missed.CV` for Power denominator |
| Standalone Power/FDR | `calculate_simrep_performance()` runs correctly; Power < 1 for at least one replicate |


### Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose | Required By |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `TEST_DB_DIR` | Path to pipeline Parquet database (`{outputDir}/db/`) | All integration tests |
| `TEST_WORK_DIR` | Path to Nextflow work directory (`.nf-work/`) | `test-cross_validation.R` |
| `TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT` | Path to `simulation_assessment_results.tsv` (legacy path) | `test-assessment_cross_validation.R` |
| `TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT` | Path to `db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv` (DB path) | `test-assessment_cross_validation.R`, `test-cv_pool.R` |
| `TEST_POPULATION` | Expected population name for single-population assertions | `test-db_structure.R` (default: `ce.test.200strains`) |
| `TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS` | Expected `nrow(mappings_metadata)` | `test-db_structure.R` (default: `4`) |
| `TEST_EXPECTED_MARKER_SETS` | Expected `nrow(marker_set_metadata)` | `test-db_structure.R` (default: `1`) |
| `TEST_EXPECTED_POPULATIONS` | Comma-separated list of all expected population names | `test-db_structure.R` (enables multi-population assertions) |
| `TEST_CV_POOL` | Set to `true` to activate cv_pool-specific assertions | `test-cv_pool.R` |


### Collecting Integration Test Data

Integration tests require pipeline output collected by `tests/collect_test_data.sh`.
Each profile stores artifacts in its own subdirectory so outputs can be tested independently:

```bash
# --- Default test profile (fixed architecture, 1 population) ---
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh
# Artifacts: tests/integration_data/test/

# Run integration tests
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/.nf-work \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

```bash
# --- test_variable profile (8 nQTL × 4 h² × 2 reps = 256 mappings) ---
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_variable
# Artifacts: tests/integration_data/test_variable/

TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_variable/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/.nf-work \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_variable/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_variable/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS=256 \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

```bash
# --- test_three_species profile (3 species × 3 populations = 9 groups, 36 mappings) ---
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_three_species
# Artifacts: tests/integration_data/test_three_species/

TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_three_species/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/.nf-work \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_three_species/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_three_species/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS=36 \
TEST_EXPECTED_MARKER_SETS=9 \
TEST_POPULATION=ce.test \
TEST_EXPECTED_POPULATIONS=ce.test,ce.test.popA,ce.test.popB,cb.test,cb.test.popA,cb.test.popB,ct.test,ct.test.popA,ct.test.popB \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

```bash
# --- test_cv_pool profile (cv_maf=0.01 < ms_maf=0.05; no legacy assess) ---
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_cv_pool --no-legacy
# Artifacts: tests/integration_data/test_cv_pool/
# Note: do NOT set TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT for this profile

TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_cv_pool/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/.nf-work \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_cv_pool/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_CV_POOL=true \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
```

The `--collect-only` and `--clean` flags work with all profiles:

```bash
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_variable --collect-only  # collect without re-running
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_variable --clean         # clean then run
```

Docker must be available for all local integration test collection runs. Integration
test files skip automatically when their required environment variables are not set.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Related pages
- To collect integration test data on the HPC: [HPC End-to-End Testing](testing-hpc.qmd)
- For cross-validation and output validation tests: [Output Validation](testing-output-validation.qmd)
:::
